column1: # say-it community pronunciation dictionary ‚ÄĒ General American only.
#   2. ipa                primary GenAm IPA, with stress marks (for display + future SSML backends)
#   3. respelling_us      English-like phonetic respelling, fed verbatim to macOS `say` (the path that actually works today)
#   5. alt_respelling_us  `|`-separated alternative respellings, paired with alt_ipa by index, or empty
#   6. source_url         best citation: documented pronunciation page, official FAQ, Wikipedia anchor, etc. Leave empty if unverified.
#   7. source_label       human-readable source label (e.g. "GNU Project official", "Wikipedia ¬ß Pronunciation")
#   8. category           project | product | cli-tool | tool | cs-term | acronym | abbreviation
#                         Only use `creator-clarified` when source_url documents a creator quote / official FAQ entry.
# Why English respelling rather than IPA/PHON phonemes?
#   macOS `say` (2026 era) does not parse `[[inpt PHON]]` markup or SSML `<phoneme>` tags ‚ÄĒ both are read as literal
#   text. So we feed plain English-like spellings that the TTS's internal letter-to-sound rules render acceptably.
#   The `ipa` column is retained for display, for future cloud TTS, and for the Windows SAPI backend (which DOES
#   - All entries are best-effort and welcome PRs to improve. Confidence should reflect what the source URL actually
#     proves, not what you believe to be true. When in doubt, mark `community-consensus` and let a future PR upgrade

column10: K8s community readings vary widely. "koob-control" is heard from Kelsey Hightower and many maintainers; "cube-cuddle" is the running meme; some say it letter-by-letter.
engine-x is the documented reading from the official site.
Wilhite (creator): "It's pronounced JIF, not GIF." Community remains split.
Crockford uses "JAY-son"; "JEE-son" is also widespread. Wikipedia notes the variance.
GOOEY is mainstream (matches Mandarin śēÖśĄŹ sound); "G-U-I" letter-by-letter is RMS/old-school; "gwee" is rare.
One syllable, hard G, rhymes with "new". Page is a dedicated pronunciation FAQ.
kates (8 letters between K and s) ‚ÄĒ community shorthand.
gra-FAH-na is the most common community reading.
KAY-native ‚ÄĒ the K is voiced. (Heard from project maintainers in talks; needs a citable source.)
RED-iss. (Heard from Salvatore Sanfilippo in interviews; citable source TBD.)
Linus's own pronunciation is "LIN-ux" (short I, schwa). Wikipedia documents the variants.
DEB-ee-uhn ‚ÄĒ portmanteau of Debra + Ian Murdock, documented on the about page.
FAQ documents "JANG-go" ‚ÄĒ silent D, like jazz guitarist Django Reinhardt.
view ‚ÄĒ one syllable. (Heard from Evan You in talks; needs a citable source.)
veet ‚ÄĒ French for "quick". (Heard from Evan You in talks; needs a citable source.)
pie-DAN-tic. (Heard from Samuel Colvin in podcasts; needs a citable source.)
Lamport: "lay-tek" (or "lah-tek"); never "lay-teks". Documented on the about page.
Knuth: like "tech" ‚ÄĒ the X is a Greek chi.
char (rhymes with "car") in C/C++ camp; "care" and "kar" also used.
JOO-pi-tur ‚ÄĒ named after Jupiter (planet) + Julia, Python, R.
One syllable, rhymes with "fraud". Anthropic's LLM family.
jacks, one syllable. Google's autograd library.
QUADRANT ‚ÄĒ common reading; project FAQ TBD as a citable source.
quick ‚ÄĒ yes, intentional. Heard from creator Misko Hevery.
shad-C-N ‚ÄĒ common community reading; creator clarification TBD as a citable source.
German for "state": "TSOO-shtund" in German; most English speakers say "ZOO-stund". Creator clarification TBD.
NEE-oh-vim is the most common community reading.
Rich Hickey has said it sounds like "closure". Community reading.
LOO-ah ‚ÄĒ Portuguese for "moon". Documented on the about page.
